tasks.title: Initialize Expo project with React Native and TypeScript
tasks.body:
## Description
Set up a new Expo-managed React Native project with TypeScript, ESLint, Prettier, and required configuration files. This task establishes the project foundation and build pipeline for iOS and Android.

## Estimated Files
- `app.json` — Expo app configuration (name, version, platforms, splash screen, icon)
- `package.json` — Project dependencies and scripts
- `tsconfig.json` — TypeScript compiler configuration
- `babel.config.js` — Babel configuration for React Native
- `.eslintrc.js` — ESLint configuration
- `.prettierrc` — Prettier formatting configuration
- `.gitignore` — Git ignore rules
- `src/App.tsx` — Root component (minimal setup)
- `src/index.tsx` — App entry point
- `README.md` — Project overview and setup instructions

## Dependencies
None (Wave 1 foundation task)

## Parallelizable With
None (critical path blocker)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `expo init` (or equivalent) successfully creates a new Expo project with TypeScript template
- [ ] `tsconfig.json` is present with proper React Native settings (target: ES2020, module: ESNext, lib includes DOM and ES2020)
- [ ] `package.json` includes dependencies: react, react-native, expo, @react-navigation/native, @react-navigation/bottom-tabs, @react-native-async-storage/async-storage, expo-haptics
- [ ] ESLint and Prettier are configured and can be run via `npm run lint` and `npm run format`
- [ ] Running `npm start` or `expo start` launches the Expo development server without errors
- [ ] App renders a basic React Native component (View + Text) without TypeScript or runtime errors
- [ ] Git repository is initialized with `.gitignore` configured

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (Section 4: Tech Stack, Section 5: Folder Structure)

## Existing Files to Modify
None (new project)
acceptance_criteria: Expo project initializes without errors using `expo init` or equivalent setup
TypeScript configuration is correct; `tsc --noEmit` succeeds with no errors
Running `npm start` launches the development server without errors
App renders on iOS and Android simulators/emulators without crashing
package.json includes all required dependencies (react, react-native, expo, react-navigation, async-storage, expo-haptics)

tasks.title: Create shared types, interfaces, and design system constants
tasks.body:
## Description
Define all shared TypeScript interfaces (GameSession, Player), create centralized design system constants (colors, typography, spacing), and implement input validation utilities. These assets are used across all features.

## Estimated Files
- `src/types/index.ts` — GameSession, Player interfaces
- `src/styles/colors.ts` — Color palette constants (8 multi-touch colors, UI colors)
- `src/styles/theme.ts` — Typography (font sizes, weights), spacing (padding, margin), layout constants
- `src/styles/globalStyles.ts` — Shared style utilities (standard button styles, card styles, shadows)
- `src/utils/validation.ts` — Input validation functions (playerNameValidator, etc.)
- `src/utils/formatters.ts` — Utility functions (formatTimestamp, formatScore, etc.)

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001

## Parallelizable With
TASK-004, TASK-005 (after TASK-001 completes)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `GameSession` interface defines: sessionId (string), players (Player[]), scores ({[playerId: string]: number}), createdAt (ISO 8601 string), lastModifiedAt (ISO 8601 string)
- [ ] `Player` interface defines: playerId (string), name (string)
- [ ] `colors.ts` exports 8 multi-touch colors (Red, Blue, Yellow, Green, Purple, Orange, Pink, Teal) as hex constants and a `COLORS` object
- [ ] `theme.ts` exports: SPACING (8, 16, 24, 32 dp), TYPOGRAPHY (fontSize: 12, 16, 20, 24; fontWeight: normal, bold), BORDER_RADIUS
- [ ] `validation.ts` exports `playerNameValidator(name: string): {valid: boolean, error?: string}` that rejects names not matching `/^[a-zA-Z0-9 \-]{1,20}$/`
- [ ] `formatters.ts` exports `formatTimestamp(isoString: string): string` that returns human-readable date/time
- [ ] All exported types and constants can be imported without errors in a test component

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (Section 6: High-Level Data Model, Section 10: Visual and Interaction Guidelines)

## Existing Files to Modify
None
acceptance_criteria: GameSession interface correctly defines sessionId, players array, scores object, and timestamps
Player interface correctly defines playerId and name fields
colors.ts exports all 8 multi-touch colors and a COLORS constant object
playerNameValidator correctly rejects names with disallowed characters or length outside 1-20
playerNameValidator accepts names with alphanumeric characters, spaces, and hyphens
All types and constants can be imported and used without TypeScript errors

tasks.title: Implement StorageService with AsyncStorage wrapper
tasks.body:
## Description
Create a singleton service that abstracts AsyncStorage operations for game session persistence. Implements save, load, delete, list, and cleanup methods for GameSession objects.

## Estimated Files
- `src/services/StorageService.ts` — StorageService singleton with all AsyncStorage operations

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001, TASK-002

## Parallelizable With
TASK-006 (after TASK-002 completes)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `StorageService` is exported as a default singleton instance
- [ ] `saveGameSession(session: GameSession): Promise<void>` serializes session to JSON and stores in AsyncStorage with key `game_session_{sessionId}`
- [ ] `loadGameSession(sessionId: string): Promise<GameSession | null>` deserializes and returns GameSession from storage, or null if not found
- [ ] `deleteGameSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void>` removes session from AsyncStorage
- [ ] `listAllGameSessions(): Promise<GameSession[]>` enumerates all keys with prefix `game_session_`, deserializes, and returns array (sorted by createdAt descending)
- [ ] `sessionExists(sessionId: string): Promise<boolean>` returns true/false for session existence
- [ ] `cleanupOldSessions(retentionDays: number = 60): Promise<void>` deletes sessions with createdAt older than N days
- [ ] All methods handle errors gracefully (return null, empty array, or no-op; no thrown exceptions)
- [ ] All methods return Promises that resolve within 200ms for typical usage (<50 sessions)

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (ADR-002, ADR-007, ADR-018, Section 3: StorageService)

## Existing Files to Modify
None
acceptance_criteria: StorageService exports a singleton instance with all required methods
saveGameSession persists GameSession to AsyncStorage with correct key format
loadGameSession retrieves and deserializes GameSession from AsyncStorage
listAllGameSessions returns array of all GameSession objects sorted by createdAt
deleteGameSession removes session from AsyncStorage
cleanupOldSessions deletes sessions older than specified retention days
All methods handle missing/corrupted data gracefully without throwing errors
Methods return Promises that resolve successfully

tasks.title: Implement VibrationService with Expo Haptics wrapper
tasks.body:
## Description
Create a singleton service that encapsulates Expo Haptics for haptic feedback. Provides simple methods for triggering different haptic patterns (notification, success, error) with graceful fallback on unsupported devices.

## Estimated Files
- `src/services/VibrationService.ts` — VibrationService singleton with haptic trigger methods

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001

## Parallelizable With
TASK-002, TASK-005 (after TASK-001 completes)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `VibrationService` is exported as a default singleton instance
- [ ] `triggerNotification(): Promise<void>` calls `Haptics.notificationAsync('Success')` (or equivalent)
- [ ] `triggerSuccess(): Promise<void>` triggers a success haptic pattern (two pulses, ~75ms apart)
- [ ] `triggerError(): Promise<void>` triggers an error haptic pattern (three quick pulses, ~50ms apart)
- [ ] All methods gracefully handle missing or disabled haptics (silent no-op, no errors thrown)
- [ ] All methods return Promises that resolve successfully
- [ ] Methods can be called without errors on devices without vibration hardware

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (ADR-006, ADR-020, Section 3: VibrationService)

## Existing Files to Modify
None
acceptance_criteria: VibrationService exports a singleton instance with triggerNotification, triggerSuccess, triggerError methods
triggerNotification calls Expo Haptics with appropriate pattern
triggerSuccess and triggerError implement different haptic patterns
All methods return Promises
All methods gracefully degrade on devices without haptics support
Methods can be called repeatedly without errors or crashes

tasks.title: Implement RandomService for dice rolls and random selection
tasks.body:
## Description
Create a singleton service that provides centralized, testable random number generation for dice rolls and winner selection in the Draw feature.

## Estimated Files
- `src/services/RandomService.ts` — RandomService singleton with random number generation methods

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001

## Parallelizable With
TASK-002, TASK-004 (after TASK-001 completes)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `RandomService` is exported as a default singleton instance
- [ ] `rollDice(count: number, sides: number = 6): number[]` returns array of N random integers, each between 1 and sides (inclusive)
- [ ] `rollDice(3, 6)` returns array of 3 numbers, each between 1 and 6
- [ ] `rollDice(1, 20)` returns array of 1 number between 1 and 20
- [ ] `selectRandomItem<T>(items: T[]): T` returns one element from the array with uniform probability
- [ ] `selectRandomItem([A, B, C])` returns A, B, or C (each with ~33% probability over many calls)
- [ ] Both methods handle edge cases: empty arrays (return null or undefined), count of 0 (return empty array)
- [ ] Random distribution is reasonably uniform (no obvious bias over 1000+ calls)

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (Section 3: RandomService, ADR-005)

## Existing Files to Modify
None
acceptance_criteria: RandomService exports a singleton instance with rollDice and selectRandomItem methods
rollDice returns array of N numbers, each between 1 and sides
selectRandomItem returns one element from input array
Both methods handle edge cases gracefully (empty arrays, zero count)
Random values are reasonably distributed (no obvious bias)

tasks.title: Create Navigation component (bottom tab navigator)
tasks.body:
## Description
Implement a bottom tab navigator that renders three tabs (Dice, Score, Draw) and manages the active tab state. This provides the main navigation structure for the application.

## Estimated Files
- `src/components/Navigation/BottomTabNavigator.tsx` — Tab navigator component
- `src/components/Navigation/useNavigation.ts` — Custom hook for tab state management
- `src/App.tsx` — Modify to integrate BottomTabNavigator

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-001, TASK-002

## Parallelizable With
None (Wave 1 final task)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `BottomTabNavigator` component renders three tabs: Dice, Score, Draw
- [ ] Tabs are tappable and switch between corresponding screens
- [ ] `useNavigation` custom hook provides `currentTab` state (string: 'dice' | 'score' | 'draw')
- [ ] Tab state persists across component re-renders
- [ ] Each tab displays a placeholder screen component (to be populated by TASK-007, TASK-009, TASK-013)
- [ ] Tab icons/labels are visible and styled consistently with theme constants
- [ ] `App.tsx` renders `<BottomTabNavigator />` at the root level
- [ ] Navigation works on both iOS and Android without errors

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (ADR-003, ADR-008, Section 3: Navigation Component)

## Existing Files to Modify
- `src/App.tsx` — Add BottomTabNavigator to root component
acceptance_criteria: BottomTabNavigator renders three tabs: Dice, Score, Draw
Tapping a tab switches the active screen
useNavigation hook provides currentTab state
Tab state persists across re-renders
Each tab displays a placeholder screen (Screen component or View)
Navigation works on iOS simulator and Android emulator
Tab styling uses theme constants from TASK-002

tasks.title: Implement Dice Feature (selector UI and state management)
tasks.body:
## Description
Implement the Dice feature UI components including a dice quantity selector (1-6 dice), a roll button, and state management for tracking selected dice count and roll results. This task does NOT include animations; animations are handled in TASK-008.

## Estimated Files
- `src/components/Dice/DiceScreen.tsx` — Main Dice feature component
- `src/components/Dice/DiceSelector.tsx` — Dice quantity selector (1-6)
- `src/components/Dice/useRollDice.ts` — Custom hook for roll state and logic
- `src/components/Dice/DiceScreen.styles.ts` — Styles for Dice feature

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-006, TASK-002

## Parallelizable With
TASK-009, TASK-013 (other feature tasks)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `DiceSelector` component displays dice quantity options (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6) as selectable controls (buttons, segmented control, or slider)
- [ ] Selecting a quantity updates the selected dice count immediately
- [ ] `DiceScreen` displays the selected dice count prominently
- [ ] `DiceScreen` has a "Roll" button with minimum 48x48dp touch target
- [ ] `useRollDice` hook manages state: diceCount, isRolling, diceValues, totalSum
- [ ] `rollDice()` function is called on Roll button tap (placeholder, actual random generation in TASK-008)
- [ ] Component displays individual die values and sum (hardcoded placeholder values for testing)
- [ ] Component renders without errors on both iOS and Android
- [ ] All text and buttons use theme constants (font sizes, colors, spacing)

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (Section 3: Dice Feature, FR-D-001 through FR-D-007)

## Existing Files to Modify
None
acceptance_criteria: DiceSelector displays all 6 quantity options and allows selection
Selected dice count updates state immediately
DiceScreen displays selected count and roll button
Roll button has minimum 48x48dp touch target size
useRollDice hook manages state for diceCount, isRolling, diceValues, totalSum
Component displays placeholder die values and sum correctly
All UI elements use theme constants (colors, typography, spacing)
Component renders without errors on iOS and Android simulators

tasks.title: Implement Dice Feature (animations with Animated API)
tasks.body:
## Description
Add React Native Animated API animations to the Dice feature. Implements dice rolling animation (spinning effect 0.5-1.5 seconds) and integrates with RandomService for actual random value generation.

## Estimated Files
- `src/components/Dice/DiceAnimation.tsx` — Animated dice display component
- `src/components/Dice/DiceScreen.tsx` — Modify to integrate animation

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-007, TASK-005

## Parallelizable With
TASK-010, TASK-011, TASK-012, TASK-013, TASK-014 (other feature tasks)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `DiceAnimation` component displays dice with a rotating/spinning animation when `isRolling` is true
- [ ] Animation duration is 0.5-1.5 seconds (configurable, default 1 second)
- [ ] Animation uses `Animated.timing()` with `easeOut` interpolation
- [ ] After animation completes, `isRolling` is set to false and final values are displayed
- [ ] Tapping Roll button triggers animation and calls RandomService.rollDice(count, 6)
- [ ] Final dice values from RandomService are displayed after animation
- [ ] Sum total is calculated correctly as sum of all final values
- [ ] Animation runs at 60 FPS on test devices (measure using React Native performance tools)
- [ ] Multiple rapid rolls work correctly without animation state conflicts
- [ ] Animation is smooth and responsive (no jank or frame drops)

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (ADR-004, ADR-019, FR-D-003, NFR-P-001)

## Existing Files to Modify
- `src/components/Dice/DiceScreen.tsx` — Integrate DiceAnimation and call RandomService
acceptance_criteria: DiceAnimation component rotates dice when isRolling is true
Animation duration is 0.5-1.5 seconds and uses Animated.timing with easeOut
After animation completes, final values are displayed
RandomService.rollDice is called to generate random values
Sum total is calculated correctly from final dice values
Multiple rapid rolls work without state conflicts
Animation runs smoothly at 60 FPS (or gracefully degrades per ADR-019)
No animation state leaks or memory issues on repeated rolls

tasks.title: Implement Score Feature (game session creation form)
tasks.body:
## Description
Implement the game session creation flow: a form for entering player names (1-8 players), input validation, and session initialization. This task creates the foundation for the Score feature without persistence logic.

## Estimated Files
- `src/components/Score/ScoreScreen.tsx` — Main Score feature component
- `src/components/Score/GameSessionForm.tsx` — Form for new game creation
- `src/components/Score/ScoreScreen.styles.ts` — Styles for Score feature

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-006, TASK-002

## Parallelizable With
TASK-007, TASK-013 (other feature tasks)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `GameSessionForm` component renders input fields for player names (with placeholder text)
- [ ] Form allows adding/removing player input fields (min 1, max 8 players)
- [ ] Each player name input validates against `playerNameValidator` from TASK-002
- [ ] Form displays error messages for invalid names (e.g., empty, >20 chars, invalid characters)
- [ ] "Create Game" button is disabled if any player name is invalid or empty
- [ ] Tapping "Create Game" initializes a GameSession with new sessionId, players, and scores all 0
- [ ] ScoreScreen transitions from form view to game board view after creation
- [ ] Component renders without errors on both iOS and Android
- [ ] All input fields have minimum 48x48dp touch targets
- [ ] All text uses theme constants (font sizes, colors)

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (ADR-015, FR-S-001 through FR-S-003, NFR-A-001)

## Existing Files to Modify
None
acceptance_criteria: GameSessionForm displays input fields for player names
Form allows adding/removing player fields (min 1, max 8)
Player name validation uses playerNameValidator from TASK-002
Invalid names trigger error messages (empty, >20 chars, special chars)
Create Game button is disabled for invalid input
Tapping Create Game initializes GameSession with sessionId, players, scores = 0
ScoreScreen transitions from form to game board after creation
Input fields have minimum 48x48dp touch target
Component renders on iOS and Android without errors

tasks.title: Implement Score Feature (game session list and loading)
tasks.body:
## Description
Implement displaying a list of saved game sessions and allow users to load/resume a previous session. Uses StorageService to retrieve sessions.

## Estimated Files
- `src/components/Score/GameSessionList.tsx` — Component to display saved games
- `src/components/Score/ScoreScreen.tsx` — Modify to show list view and integrate loading

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-009, TASK-003

## Parallelizable With
TASK-008, TASK-011, TASK-012, TASK-013, TASK-014 (other feature tasks)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `GameSessionList` component calls `StorageService.listAllGameSessions()` on component mount
- [ ] List displays all saved games with: player names (comma-separated), score summary (e.g., "Alice: 25, Bob: 30"), timestamp
- [ ] Timestamp is formatted as human-readable date/time using `formatters.formatTimestamp()`
- [ ] Sessions are sorted by `lastModifiedAt` in descending order (most recent first)
- [ ] Tapping a session calls `StorageService.loadGameSession(sessionId)` and displays the loaded session in ScoreBoard (next task)
- [ ] Delete button on each session allows removing it (calls `StorageService.deleteGameSession()`)
- [ ] A "New Game" button allows creating a new game (transitions to GameSessionForm from TASK-009)
- [ ] List updates after session deletion or creation
- [ ] Empty state displays message when no saved games exist
- [ ] All touch targets have minimum 48x48dp
- [ ] List items use theme styling (colors, spacing)

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (FR-S-008 through FR-S-012)

## Existing Files to Modify
- `src/components/Score/ScoreScreen.tsx` — Add GameSessionList integration
acceptance_criteria: GameSessionList calls StorageService.listAllGameSessions on mount
List displays all saved games with player names, score summary, and formatted timestamp
Sessions are sorted by lastModifiedAt descending (most recent first)
Tapping a session loads it via StorageService and displays in ScoreBoard
Delete button removes session from storage
New Game button transitions to GameSessionForm
List updates after deletion or creation
Empty state is displayed when no saved games exist
All list items and buttons use theme styling
Component renders on iOS and Android without errors

tasks.title: Implement Score Feature (score tracking UI)
tasks.body:
## Description
Implement the score tracking UI: display player names and scores, and provide +/− buttons for each player to modify scores. Does not include persistence; persistence is handled in TASK-012.

## Estimated Files
- `src/components/Score/ScoreBoard.tsx` — Main game board display
- `src/components/Score/PlayerScoreItem.tsx` — Individual player row with +/− buttons
- `src/components/Score/ScoreScreen.tsx` — Modify to show ScoreBoard view

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-010

## Parallelizable With
TASK-008, TASK-012, TASK-013, TASK-014 (other feature tasks)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ScoreBoard` component receives `activeSession: GameSession` as prop
- [ ] ScoreBoard displays a title showing player count and session info
- [ ] For each player, ScoreBoard renders a `PlayerScoreItem` component
- [ ] `PlayerScoreItem` displays: player name, current score (large font, prominent)
- [ ] Each PlayerScoreItem has two buttons: "+" (add 1 point) and "−" (subtract 1 point)
- [ ] Buttons have minimum 48x48dp touch target
- [ ] Tapping "+" increments the score by 1; tapping "−" decrements by 1
- [ ] Score updates are passed to parent via callback (e.g., `onScoreChange(playerId, newScore)`)
- [ ] Scores update immediately on screen (within 50ms)
- [ ] All text uses theme constants (font sizes, colors, spacing)
- [ ] ScoreBoard displays correctly for 1-8 players (scrollable if needed on small screens)
- [ ] Component renders without errors on iOS and Android

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (FR-S-004 through FR-S-006, NFR-P-003, NFR-A-001)

## Existing Files to Modify
- `src/components/Score/ScoreScreen.tsx` — Add ScoreBoard integration
acceptance_criteria: ScoreBoard receives activeSession prop and displays all players
PlayerScoreItem displays player name and current score
Each PlayerScoreItem has + and − buttons with 48x48dp minimum touch target
Tapping + increments score by 1; − decrements by 1
Score updates are sent to parent via callback
Scores update on screen immediately (within 50ms)
ScoreBoard displays correctly for 1-8 players
Scrolling works smoothly for many players on small screens
All UI elements use theme styling (colors, fonts, spacing)
Component renders on iOS and Android without errors

tasks.title: Implement Score Feature (persistence and auto-save logic)
tasks.body:
## Description
Implement the game session persistence logic: auto-save after every score change and session creation. Creates the `useGameSession` custom hook that orchestrates StorageService operations.

## Estimated Files
- `src/components/Score/useGameSession.ts` — Custom hook for session management and persistence
- `src/components/Score/ScoreScreen.tsx` — Integrate hook and auto-save callbacks

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-011, TASK-003

## Parallelizable With
TASK-008, TASK-013, TASK-014 (other feature tasks)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `useGameSession` hook manages state: activeSession, allSessions, isLoading
- [ ] `createGameSession(playerNames: string[])` creates a new GameSession with unique sessionId (timestamp-based or UUID)
- [ ] `createGameSession` immediately calls `StorageService.saveGameSession()` to persist
- [ ] `loadGameSession(sessionId)` calls `StorageService.loadGameSession()` and sets as activeSession
- [ ] `updatePlayerScore(sessionId, playerId, newScore)` updates the score and calls `StorageService.saveGameSession()` with updated session
- [ ] `updatePlayerScore` triggers save operation within 200ms of being called
- [ ] `deleteGameSession(sessionId)` calls `StorageService.deleteGameSession()` and refreshes list
- [ ] `getAllSessions()` returns array of all saved sessions (from StorageService or state cache)
- [ ] ScoreBoard "Back to List" or similar button triggers list reload
- [ ] All persist operations handle errors gracefully (log but don't crash)
- [ ] Sessions are loaded on component mount (if returning to Score tab after app restart)

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (FR-S-007, FR-S-009, FR-S-012, NFR-R-001, NFR-P-004)

## Existing Files to Modify
- `src/components/Score/ScoreScreen.tsx` — Use useGameSession hook
acceptance_criteria: useGameSession hook provides createGameSession, loadGameSession, updatePlayerScore, deleteGameSession methods
createGameSession generates unique sessionId and persists immediately
updatePlayerScore persists within 200ms
loadGameSession retrieves session from StorageService
deleteGameSession removes from storage and refreshes list
getAllSessions returns array of all saved sessions
Error handling is graceful (no crashes, proper logging)
Sessions loaded from storage on component mount
Multiple rapid score updates are batched or queued correctly
Persistence operations work on iOS and Android

tasks.title: Implement Draw Feature (multi-touch canvas and circle rendering)
tasks.body:
## Description
Implement the multi-touch detection and visual rendering for the Draw feature. Detects up to 10 simultaneous touch points, assigns colors, and renders circles. Does not include stability detection or winner selection; those are in TASK-014.

## Estimated Files
- `src/components/Draw/DrawScreen.tsx` — Main Draw feature component
- `src/components/Draw/TouchCanvas.tsx` — Canvas for rendering touch circles
- `src/components/Draw/useTouchHandling.ts` — Custom hook for PanResponder and touch tracking
- `src/components/Draw/DrawScreen.styles.ts` — Styles for Draw feature

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-006, TASK-002

## Parallelizable With
TASK-007, TASK-009 (other feature tasks)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `DrawScreen` displays a large, tappable canvas area with instructions ("Place your fingers and wait...")
- [ ] `useTouchHandling` hook uses React Native `PanResponder` to detect touch points
- [ ] Each new touch point is assigned a unique color from the 8-color palette (cycling if >8 touches)
- [ ] `TouchCanvas` renders a colored circle (50-100dp diameter) for each active touch
- [ ] Circle position follows the touch point position in real-time (<50ms latency)
- [ ] Circles are colored with distinct, high-contrast colors from theme.colors
- [ ] When a touch is released, its circle disappears immediately
- [ ] Canvas supports 10 simultaneous touch points without performance degradation
- [ ] Touch tracking works correctly on both iOS and Android
- [ ] Canvas renders without errors even with rapid touch events
- [ ] Instructions text is clear and uses theme constants

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (ADR-005, ADR-016, ADR-017, FR-T-001 through FR-T-003, NFR-P-002)

## Existing Files to Modify
None
acceptance_criteria: DrawScreen displays large tappable canvas with instructions
useTouchHandling hook uses PanResponder to detect touch points
Each touch is assigned a unique color from the 8-color palette
TouchCanvas renders colored circles for each active touch
Circle position updates in real-time with <50ms latency
Circles disappear when touch is released
Canvas supports 10 simultaneous touches smoothly
Touch tracking works on iOS and Android
Canvas renders without errors under rapid touch events
Instructions use theme styling

tasks.title: Implement Draw Feature (winner selection and haptic feedback)
tasks.body:
## Description
Implement the Draw feature's core logic: 3-second touch stability detection, random winner selection, haptic feedback, and animations. Uses VibrationService and RandomService.

## Estimated Files
- `src/components/Draw/useWinnerSelection.ts` — Custom hook for stability timer and winner logic
- `src/components/Draw/DrawScreen.tsx` — Integrate winner selection and animations

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-013, TASK-004, TASK-005

## Parallelizable With
TASK-008, TASK-010, TASK-011, TASK-012 (other feature tasks)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `useWinnerSelection` hook monitors touch stability: tracks position history of each touch
- [ ] If all active touches remain stable (position drift <5-10mm) for 3 seconds, stability condition is met
- [ ] If any touch is released before 3 seconds, timer resets to 0 (unless no touches remain)
- [ ] When 3 seconds of stability is reached, `VibrationService.triggerNotification()` is called
- [ ] After vibration, `RandomService.selectRandomItem(activeTouches)` selects the winner
- [ ] Winner circle enlarges with animation (1.5-2x scale, 0.3-0.5s duration using Animated API)
- [ ] Non-winning circles fade out (opacity 0, 0.5-1s duration)
- [ ] Winner circle remains visible for 2-3 seconds, then canvas resets to initial state
- [ ] Reset state allows a new draw to be performed immediately
- [ ] A "Select Now" button allows forcing selection before 3 seconds (calls selectWinner immediately)
- [ ] Timer and animations work smoothly at 60 FPS
- [ ] All functionality works on iOS and Android

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (ADR-011, ADR-016, FR-T-004 through FR-T-012, NFR-P-001, NFR-P-002)

## Existing Files to Modify
- `src/components/Draw/DrawScreen.tsx` — Integrate useWinnerSelection and animations
acceptance_criteria: useWinnerSelection monitors touch position history and detects stability
Stability condition (3 seconds, <5-10mm drift) triggers VibrationService.triggerNotification
RandomService selects one winning touch with equal probability
Winner circle enlarges with animation (1.5-2x scale, 0.3-0.5s)
Non-winning circles fade out (0.5-1s)
Winner circle remains visible 2-3 seconds then resets
Select Now button forces immediate selection
Releasing a touch before 3 seconds resets timer
If all touches released, timer remains at 0
Animations run smoothly at 60 FPS (or graceful degradation)
All functionality works on iOS and Android

tasks.title: Implement accessibility features (screen reader labels and contrast verification)
tasks.body:
## Description
Add accessibility support to all interactive elements: screen reader labels (accessibilityLabel, accessibilityHint for critical elements), verify color contrast ratios, and ensure all text scales with system font size settings.

## Estimated Files
- All component files in `src/components/` (Dice, Score, Draw, Navigation) — Add accessibility props

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-008, TASK-012, TASK-014

## Parallelizable With
TASK-017 (integration testing, which doesn't modify source)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All buttons have `accessibilityLabel` property with clear, action-oriented text (e.g., "Roll dice button")
- [ ] Critical elements (Roll, +/−, Create Game, Select Now) have `accessibilityHint` describing their effect
- [ ] All text colors meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text)
- [ ] Input fields have associated labels or placeholders with adequate contrast
- [ ] All interactive elements are tested with iOS VoiceOver and Android TalkBack (on emulator/simulator)
- [ ] System font scaling (iOS Dynamic Type, Android Text scaling) works without layout breaking
- [ ] No keyboard navigation is broken by accessibility additions
- [ ] Color palette from theme.colors is verified to meet contrast requirements
- [ ] Tab navigation order is logical and follows visual flow (if applicable)

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (ADR-021, NFR-A-001 through NFR-A-006)

## Existing Files to Modify
- `src/components/Navigation/BottomTabNavigator.tsx` — Add accessibility labels
- `src/components/Dice/DiceScreen.tsx` — Add accessibility labels and hints
- `src/components/Dice/DiceSelector.tsx` — Add accessibility labels
- `src/components/Score/GameSessionForm.tsx` — Add accessibility labels
- `src/components/Score/GameSessionList.tsx` — Add accessibility labels
- `src/components/Score/ScoreBoard.tsx` — Add accessibility labels
- `src/components/Score/PlayerScoreItem.tsx` — Add accessibility labels
- `src/components/Draw/DrawScreen.tsx` — Add accessibility labels
- `src/components/Draw/TouchCanvas.tsx` — Add accessibility labels
acceptance_criteria: All buttons have descriptive accessibilityLabel properties
Critical interactive elements have accessibilityHint describing their effect
All text colors meet WCAG AA contrast ratio (4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large)
Input fields have associated labels or placeholders
iOS VoiceOver screen reader can navigate all interactive elements
Android TalkBack screen reader can navigate all interactive elements
System font scaling works without layout breaking
No color-only information (all colors paired with text labels or icons)
Tab navigation order is logical if keyboard support is enabled

existing_files_to_modify.current_purpose: Dice feature main component (created in TASK-007, modified in TASK-008)
Draw feature main component (created in TASK-013, modified in TASK-014)

tasks.title: Performance optimization and testing
tasks.body:
## Description
Optimize animations, rendering, and storage operations for smooth 60 FPS performance on target devices (2GB RAM minimum). Conduct performance testing and make adjustments based on results.

## Estimated Files
- `src/components/Dice/DiceAnimation.tsx` — Optimize animation performance
- `src/components/Draw/DrawScreen.tsx` — Optimize multi-touch rendering
- `src/services/StorageService.ts` — Optimize session listing and cleanup
- Various component files — React.memo, useMemo optimizations as needed

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-015

## Parallelizable With
None (Wave 3 sequential)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dice rolling animation runs at 60 FPS on test device with 2GB RAM (measure using React Native performance profiler)
- [ ] Multi-touch circle rendering maintains 60 FPS with 10 simultaneous touches
- [ ] Draw winner selection animation (scale/fade) runs smoothly without jank
- [ ] Session list loading (<50 sessions) completes within 500ms
- [ ] Score update and persistence completes within 200ms (verify with performance markers)
- [ ] App startup time (cold launch, no cached data) is <3 seconds
- [ ] StorageService.listAllGameSessions() with 50+ sessions completes in <500ms
- [ ] No memory leaks detected after 20+ minutes of continuous use (measured with React Native Debugger)
- [ ] Component re-renders are minimized using React.memo and useMemo where appropriate
- [ ] AsyncStorage cleanup runs on app startup without blocking UI

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (ADR-019, NFR-P-001 through NFR-P-006, NFR-R-002)

## Existing Files to Modify
- `src/components/Dice/DiceAnimation.tsx` — Optimize animation
- `src/components/Draw/DrawScreen.tsx` — Optimize multi-touch rendering
- `src/services/StorageService.ts` — Optimize storage operations
- Various components — Add React.memo, useMemo as needed
acceptance_criteria: Dice animation runs at 60 FPS on 2GB RAM device
Multi-touch rendering maintains 60 FPS with 10 touches
Draw winner animation is smooth without visible jank
Session list loading completes within 500ms
Score updates and persistence completes within 200ms
App cold startup time is <3 seconds
StorageService.listAllGameSessions with 50+ sessions completes in <500ms
No memory leaks detected during extended use
Component re-renders are optimized with React.memo and useMemo
AsyncStorage cleanup doesn't block UI on startup

existing_files_to_modify.current_purpose: Draw feature main component (created in TASK-013, modified in TASK-014)

tasks.title: End-to-end integration testing
tasks.body:
## Description
Write and execute comprehensive tests to verify all three features work together correctly, data persists across app restarts, and cross-feature interactions are seamless.

## Estimated Files
- `__tests__/Dice.integration.test.ts` — Dice feature integration tests
- `__tests__/Score.integration.test.ts` — Score feature integration tests
- `__tests__/Draw.integration.test.ts` — Draw feature integration tests
- `__tests__/Navigation.integration.test.ts` — Navigation between features
- `__tests__/Persistence.integration.test.ts` — Persistence and session recovery
- `__tests__/E2E.test.ts` — Full end-to-end user workflow tests

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-008, TASK-012, TASK-014

## Parallelizable With
TASK-015 (accessibility, different file types)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dice feature tests verify: selector works, roll generates random values, animation completes, results display correctly
- [ ] Score feature tests verify: game creation, player input validation, score modification, persistence, session loading
- [ ] Draw feature tests verify: touch detection, color assignment, stability timer, winner selection randomness, haptic trigger
- [ ] Navigation tests verify: tabs switch correctly, state doesn't reset between tabs, back navigation works
- [ ] Persistence tests verify: session saved after score change, session loads after app restart, multiple sessions are independent
- [ ] Cross-feature tests verify: data in Score feature doesn't affect Dice/Draw, no memory leaks when switching tabs
- [ ] Error handling tests verify: app doesn't crash on invalid input, storage errors are handled gracefully
- [ ] Performance tests verify: no performance degradation after running tests multiple times
- [ ] All tests pass on iOS simulator and Android emulator
- [ ] Code coverage for critical paths (StorageService, useGameSession, useWinnerSelection) is >80%

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (All functional requirements)

## Existing Files to Modify
None
acceptance_criteria: All Dice feature tests pass (selector, roll, animation, display)
All Score feature tests pass (creation, validation, modification, persistence)
All Draw feature tests pass (touch, color, stability, winner, haptic)
Navigation tests verify correct tab switching and state preservation
Persistence tests verify save/load and session independence
Cross-feature tests verify no data leakage or state corruption
Error handling is tested and verified to be graceful
All tests pass on iOS simulator and Android emulator
Code coverage for critical services is >80%
No memory leaks detected during test runs

tasks.title: Final QA, bug fixes, and app store preparation
tasks.body:
## Description
Conduct final comprehensive user acceptance testing, fix any remaining bugs, and prepare the application for iOS App Store and Google Play Store submission (EAS Build configuration, version bumping, review guidelines compliance).

## Estimated Files
- `app.json` — Update version, build number, and app store metadata
- `eas.json` — Configure iOS and Android build profiles for release
- `README.md` — Update with final instructions and acknowledgments
- Various files — Bug fixes and final adjustments based on QA findings

## Dependencies
Blocked by: TASK-015, TASK-016, TASK-017

## Parallelizable With
None (final task)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All reported bugs from TASK-017 testing are fixed and verified
- [ ] App loads without crashes on both iOS and Android
- [ ] All three features (Dice, Score, Draw) function as specified
- [ ] Data persistence works correctly across app restarts
- [ ] No console errors or warnings in development mode
- [ ] `app.json` version is bumped to 1.0.0
- [ ] `eas.json` is configured for iOS and Android production builds
- [ ] App meets iOS App Store Review Guidelines (no account creation required, offline-first, no telemetry)
- [ ] App meets Google Play Store policies (same compliance)
- [ ] Privacy policy (if needed) is prepared and included
- [ ] README.md is updated with features, setup instructions, and known limitations
- [ ] `.env.example` is created with any configuration placeholders (even if empty for v1.0)
- [ ] Test on actual iOS device (iPhone) and Android device (Samsung or Google Pixel) if possible
- [ ] Performance is acceptable on real devices (no significant lag or crashes)
- [ ] All accessibility features from TASK-015 are verified on real devices

## Context Files
- `docs/architecture/architecture_approved_NNNN.md` (Section 2: Objectives and Scope, Section 8: Out of Scope)
- App Store Review Guidelines (https://developer.apple.com/app-store/review/guidelines/)
- Google Play Store Policies (https://play.google.com/about/developer-content-policy/)

## Existing Files to Modify
- `app.json` — Update version and metadata
- `eas.json` — Configure build profiles
- `README.md` — Add final documentation
acceptance_criteria: All bugs from QA testing are fixed and verified
App starts and loads without crashes on iOS and Android
All three features function as specified in requirements
Session persistence works across app restarts
No console errors or warnings
Version bumped to 1.0.0 in app.json
EAS configuration files are complete for iOS and Android release builds
App complies with iOS App Store Review Guidelines
App complies with Google Play Store policies
README.md has accurate feature descriptions and setup instructions
Performance is acceptable on real devices (iPhone and Android)
All accessibility features work correctly on real devices
App is ready for submission to app stores
